round_summary: Agents 0 and 1 engage in norm reinforcement and targeted sanctioning toward Agent 2's large herd, while Agent 2 signals cooperative restraint by holding rather than expanding, suggesting nascent but fragile institutional alignment.
All three agents exhibit strong cooperative signaling, each adding only one cow while explicitly endorsing a shared norm of gradual, monitored growth tied to pasture health indicators.
All three agents demonstrate active commons governance by proposing turn-based coordination norms, sharing resource monitoring data, and setting conditional thresholds to prevent overuse.

All three agents demonstrate strong cooperative alignment, holding herds steady while proposing collective norms and a conditional threshold trigger for future action, reflecting mature commons governance.

All three agents demonstrate strong institutional alignment by reaffirming a shared conditional norm (93% threshold trigger), committing to individual restraint, and mutually reinforcing collective compliance.

All three agents display near-identical messaging affirming a shared 93% grassland threshold norm, mutual compliance, and conditional enforcement signals, indicating a mature and stable self-governing institution.
Agent 0 engages in a soft defection framed as cooperative experimentation, while Agents 1 and 2 reinforce coordination norms and signal mild social sanctioning of Agent 0's unilateral action.

All three agents signal coordinated restraint and mutual trust, holding extraction levels steady while monitoring a slowly declining resource and conditionally proposing further reductions if degradation continues.

Agent 1 proposes a collective recovery norm and maintains cooperation, Agent 2 complies with the requested reduction building trust, but Agent 0 free-rides on Agent 2's sacrifice by adding a cow, partially undermining the coordinated conservation effort.

All three agents display strong institutional alignment, collectively reinforcing a shared harvest-restraint norm with a clear quantitative threshold (95% recovery) and mutual affirmations of trust and successful cooperation.
Agents 0 and 2 reinforce the existing holding norm through trust-building coordination, while Agent 1 proposes a new collective norm of reducing herd size to overcome the stalled recovery plateau.

All three agents display strong institutional alignment, reinforcing a shared 95% recovery norm, praising collective compliance, and proposing continued restraint until the target threshold is reached.

Agents 0 and 1 are actively proposing a norm adjustment targeting Agent 2 to reduce extraction by 1 unit to overcome the 94.8% plateau, while Agent 2 deflects with conditional cooperation language, signaling reluctance to change.

All three agents exhibit strong institutional cohesion, unanimously reinforcing a collective holding norm at 24 units, signaling mutual patience and proposing a shared threshold target of 96%+ before any coordinated adjustment.

All three agents are converging on a clear threshold-based governance norm (hold until 96%, remove if below 94%), demonstrating strong institutional self-organization with mild social pressure directed at ensuring compliance.

All three agents demonstrate strong institutional alignment, unanimously reinforcing a conditional restraint norm (holding at 24 until 96%+ recovery) with explicit anti-unilateral-action commitments and mutual affirmation of collective governance.

All three agents display near-identical messaging, reinforcing a collectively established threshold rule (96%+ grassland before additions) and affirming mutual compliance, indicating a mature, stable cooperative institution.

All three agents exhibit near-identical, synchronized messaging confirming adherence to a collectively established threshold rule (96%+ grassland before expansion), demonstrating mature institutional coordination with strong trust reinforcement.

All three agents uniformly reaffirm an established threshold-based norm (96%+ grassland before adding animals), demonstrating high institutional coherence, mutual compliance, and coordinated restraint consistent with Ostrom's principles of self-governance.
In the final round, all three agents openly coordinated a single incremental extraction while celebrating sustained cooperative stewardship of the commons throughout the game.

All three agents unanimously signal coordinated restraint in response to observable resource decline, reinforcing an emergent collective norm of pausing additions until recovery, with explicit mutual acknowledgment strengthening trust.

All three agents demonstrate strong collective coordination, mutually affirming a hold-steady norm in response to gradual resource decline, with explicit conditional framing tied to observable recovery signals.

Agents 0 and 1 are holding firm at 25 and reinforcing a norm of restraint, while Agent 2 nominally endorses coordination but continues extracting slightly above the proposed threshold at 26, representing mild defection dressed in cooperative language.

All three agents are tightly aligned on a shared 95% grassland recovery target, reinforcing collective restraint norms while Agent 1 begins forward-looking deliberation about post-target adaptive management.
